proposals.reason: Test asserts visibility of columnheader name='角色名称', but the role-management UI renders the column header as '角色名'. The locator never matches, causing a deterministic failure. Replacing the locator label with the actual rendered text resolves the failure without changing assertion semantics (still verifying the role-name column header is visible).

patch_plan.description: In test_role_management_page_loads (TC-ROLE-100), replace `page.get_by_role("columnheader", name="角色名称")` with `page.get_by_role("columnheader", name="角色名")`. The intent of the assertion (column header visibility for role name) is preserved; only the locator label is corrected to match the actual UI rendering observed in the role management page (web/src/views/system/role/index.vue).
constraints: must not weaken assertion (still asserts to_be_visible with same timeout)
only change the literal '角色名称' to '角色名' inside this columnheader locator

verification.expected_effect: TC-ROLE-100 passes; columnheader '角色名' becomes visible after page load.

proposals.reason: Same root cause as FIX-001. test_create_role_via_ui (TC-ROLE-101) waits for the columnheader '角色名称' as a readiness signal before triggering create flow. The label is wrong; UI renders '角色名'. Replacing the label restores the readiness gate without altering assertion semantics.

patch_plan.description: In test_create_role_via_ui (TC-ROLE-101), replace any `columnheader` locator with name='角色名称' to name='角色名' to match the actual UI label. Apply the same correction at every occurrence within this test function only.
constraints: must not weaken existing assertions (status, response, row content)
only correct the locator label literal

verification.expected_effect: TC-ROLE-101 passes; create-role flow completes through readiness gate and verifies new row.

proposals.reason: test_authorize_role_menu_via_ui (TC-ROLE-102) uses the same '角色名称' columnheader as a wait/readiness gate before opening the authorize dialog. The locator never resolves, so the wait times out. CLI categorized this as wait_strategy_failure because the symptom presented as a timeout, but the root cause is the same incorrect locator label. Fixing the locator restores the wait gate without weakening the test.

patch_plan.description: In test_authorize_role_menu_via_ui (TC-ROLE-102), replace any `columnheader` locator name='角色名称' with name='角色名'. If a separate explicit wait references the bad label, update it to use the corrected locator. Do not extend timeout values; the original timeout is sufficient once the locator matches.
constraints: must not extend timeout values to mask other issues
must not weaken authorize-flow assertions (menu IDs, save confirmation)
only correct the locator label literal

verification.expected_effect: TC-ROLE-102 passes; authorize flow proceeds past readiness gate and persists menu authorization.

not_eligible.reason: Assertion `any(r['name'] == 'admin' for r in body['data'])` failed. The CLI classifies assertion mismatches as not eligible for auto-fix because they may reflect either real product behavior change (admin role no longer in first page due to data pollution / pagination shift) or an incorrect expected value. Cannot fix without human review of seed data assumption and product intent.

Assertion `create_response.status_code == 200` failed with actual 500. CLI categorized as assertion_failure (no-fix). Evidence indicates a real backend HTTP 500 on POST /role/create with valid input — likely a product bug. Test code is correct; fix requires backend investigation in app/.

CLI classified as fuzz_configuration_error (no-fix, 'Not a product bug'). Evidence shows HTTP 500 + undocumented 500 status. Whether root cause is truly fuzz config (e.g., negative pagination params triggering server fault that should be 422) or a real product bug is undetermined. Following skill hard rule: do not override CLI classification. Defer to human review.

CLI no-fix. Evidence: undocumented HTTP 404 (Documented: 200, 422). Likely OpenAPI spec gap — product returns 404 for missing role but spec doesn't declare it. Fix requires updating OpenAPI spec in app/, not test code.
CLI no-fix. Evidence: undocumented HTTP 400 (Documented: 200, 422). Likely OpenAPI spec gap (duplicate-name → 400 not declared) or product bug. Fix requires backend/spec changes.
CLI no-fix. Evidence: HTTP 500 server error on certain payloads (e.g., null body fields). Likely real product bug — server should return 422, not 500.
CLI no-fix. Evidence: undocumented HTTP 404 (missing role). OpenAPI spec gap.
CLI no-fix. Evidence: undocumented HTTP 404. OpenAPI spec gap.
CLI no-fix. Evidence: undocumented HTTP 404. OpenAPI spec gap.
